news.title: IPO rush among developers reflects better governance, but expansion must be calibrated: Brigade Group's founder
news.para: India's real estate market is booming post-pandemic with premium homes gaining traction. However, mid-income and mass housing remain key. Brigade Group's M.R. Jaishankar highlights the need for government support in affordable housing and developer caution on expansion. The sector's long-term outlook is positive, driven by economic growth and rising incomes.

news.title: Centre gives nod for ₹1,250 crore IT park in Puducherry
news.para: Puducherry is set to establish a dedicated IT Special Economic Zone (SEZ) with Union government approval, costing Rs1,250 crore. This 'Work-Live-Play' mini-city on 21.3 acres aims to retain local talent by creating 30,000 jobs and boosting service exports, transforming the region into a tech hub.

news.title: Mumbai: MHADA ‘duped of’ ₹121 crore rent in 25 years
news.para: Maharashtra Housing and Area Development Authority (MHADA) has been defrauded of over Rs 121 crore. Ten developers allegedly failed to pay rent for transit tenements for 25 years. The Kherwadi police registered a case. The Economic Offences Wing is now investigating this significant financial fraud. This case involves transit premises provided for redevelopment projects across Mumbai.

news.title: Case of cheating registered against over 150 landowners, developers in Nashik
news.para: Over 150 landowners and developers in Nashik face cheating charges for allegedly evading mandatory EWS housing reservations. An inquiry found that plots were artificially split to bypass the 20% reservation rule for projects exceeding 4,000sqm. Investigations are ongoing, with potential arrests expected.

news.title: Mayor says reprieve soon but Ghaziabad civic body claims no change in property tax hike
news.para: Ghaziabad Mayor Sunita Dayal announced a rollback of the steep property tax hike, claiming senior officials in Lucknow assured her of its non-implementation. Despite the mayor's statement, Ghaziabad Municipal Corporation officials have not received formal directives, and residents report significant tax increases under the revised structure.

news.title: Uttarakhand HC orders HRDA to withdraw permission for plotting on agricultural land
news.para: The Uttarakhand High Court has ordered the Haridwar-Roorkee Development Authority to cancel permissions for plotting and group housing on agricultural and orchard land. This directive comes after the court heard a public interest litigation alleging violations of previous court orders. Failure to comply within a week could lead to contempt proceedings.

news.title: Mumbai: BMC takes action against 6,922 properties over ₹6,000 crore tax dues
news.para: The BMC has taken strong action against property tax defaulters, attaching 6,922 properties and seizing 483. This aggressive approach has already yielded over Rs 784 crore from 2,888 owners. The municipality is also proposing to auction 210 properties to recover the remaining Rs 6,024 crore in dues.

news.title: Bengaluru PGs hit with commercial LPG shortage, tenants seek work-from-home option
news.para: Commercial LPG shortage is impacting paying guest accommodations in Bengaluru's IT corridor, forcing PG owners to reduce meal services and leading tenants to seek work-from-home options.  Areas like Whitefield and Marathahalli are experiencing a critical lack of gas cylinders, with some PGs having only a few days of stock left.

news.title: Bangalore development body is a promoter: Karnataka REAT
news.para: Karnataka Real Estate Appellate Tribunal ruled that the Bangalore Development Authority (BDA) must comply with RERA regulations, including project registration, as it acts as a promoter when developing and selling plots. The tribunal upheld a K-Rera order regarding the Nadaprabhu Kempegowda Layout, dismissing BDA's appeal. This decision impacts over 10,000 families awaiting project completion.

news.title: Builder cheated of crores with forged land documents in Sohna, one staffer held
news.para: A real estate firm in Sohna lost crores due to a land deal fraud. Forged documents were used to change agreement terms and cancel a registered General Power of Attorney. Five individuals have been arrested in connection with the case. The investigation revealed a conspiracy to create fake agreements and termination letters. One accused admitted to preparing the fraudulent documents.

news.title: Tata Sons takes Ratan Tata bungalow on lease in South Mumbai’s Colaba
news.para: The bungalow has a super built-up area of 13,200 sq ft, comprising a basement, ground floor and three upper floors, making it one of the larger standalone residential properties in the Colaba area.

news.title: Haryana RERA orders production warrant for jailed director of Vipul
news.para: Haryana Rera has issued a production warrant for Vipul Ltd director Punit Beriwala, currently in judicial custody, to appear before its adjudicating officer on March 13. This action is in connection with a homebuyer's complaint against the realty firm regarding a real estate project dispute. The warrant ensures the case proceedings can continue despite Beriwala's existing custody.

news.title: ED attaches ₹313.12 crore assets in money laundering case against Ansal Group
news.para: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) on Wednesday said it has attached immovable properties valued at Rs 313.12 crore as part of its ongoing investigation into Ansal Properties and Infrastructure Ltd (APIL) and its promoters for their involvement in criminal activities, including financial misappropriation and fraudulent diversion of funds collected from homebuyers.

news.title: Mazgaon redevelopment row: Mumbai sessions court grants anticipatory bail to developer, others
news.para: Mumbai's Session Court granted anticipatory bail to developer Munaf Vadgama and three relatives in a cheating and criminal breach of trust case. The accused were involved in a redevelopment dispute where a promised alternate shop was allegedly not provided. The court noted substantial payments made to the complainant's family, deeming custodial interrogation unnecessary.

news.title: Government identifying new site for Unity Mall: Goa tourism minister
news.para: Goa's Tourism Minister Rohan Khaunte announced the state government is identifying a new location for the Unity Mall. The previous Chimbel site was abandoned considering local community emotions. The minister stated the opposition was political. Rs 7.1 crore has been disbursed for the project. A consultant's claim is under review. The new site will be a cabinet decision.

news.title: MHADA targeting 20,000 affordable homes through cluster redevelopment in Mumbai
news.para: Mumbai is shifting to large-scale cluster redevelopment projects. This strategy aims to unlock significant land for housing and boost affordable housing supply. MHADA is prioritizing redevelopment of aging housing stock and expanding affordable housing for lower income groups. Cluster projects are expected to generate substantial housing units and premiums over the coming years.

news.title: Hubballi Dharwad civic body may soon get nod for 64,000 LED streetlights
news.para: Hubballi-Dharwad's long-awaited LED streetlight project is set to commence within weeks. After a delay, the selected agency has submitted its bank surety, clearing the path for the Rs 93.6-crore initiative. This project will replace conventional lights with 64,000 LED lamps, promising energy savings, improved safety, and a centralized monitoring system for enhanced efficiency.

news.title: Pune civic body allocates ₹954 crore for development of merged villages
news.para: Pune Municipal Corporation's draft budget allocates Rs954 crore for newly merged areas, prioritizing infrastructure development. The budget, driven by projected revenue from building permissions and property taxes, aims for inclusive growth and uniform development across the city. Funds are earmarked for roads, drainage, and a 24x7 water supply project.

news.title: Registration department mulls clearing tenant agreements from any sub-registrar office in Pune
news.para: Pune's property registration department is planning to let any sub-registrar office in the district clear tenant agreements online. This move aims to reduce delays and speed up approvals. The system is already working in Mumbai and Thane. Officials are addressing software issues in Pune.
